chapters.title: Useful design is a pillar of modern life and deserves care
chapters.text: Design is a key part of daily life, since almost everything around us is shaped by it. It shows up in many fields, from writing a poem to raising a building, painting a mural, baking a cake, or writing a concerto. We often miss how much design shapes our lives, yet it is woven into every part of them. Without careful and thoughtful design, we would lose many of the things we count on each day. Design is the conscious and intuitive effort to bring meaningful order. It follows that design has to be meaningful, but no one has set a fixed rule for what it must look like. The only test that really matters is whether it works. If an item does not work, it holds no real meaning. Six parts decide if a design is good or not. First, method, the smooth blend and best use of tools, materials, and process. Second, use, does it work and is it easy to handle. Third, need, it must meet the needs of the target audience. Fourth, telesis, it should fit the times and the conditions. Fifth, association, people should be able to relate to the design and want it. Sixth, aesthetics, it should appeal to the target audience and ideally to a wider circle as well. If you want to see how design works in the real world, you have come to the right summary. Read on to pick up some useful facts.

chapters.title: Simple choices can bring new problems, so look past short term wins
chapters.text: Society moves at a fast clip, which makes design more layered and harder to predict. Design is core to the human story, and it now goes beyond the designer intent and the buyer need. Every effort has a wide reach that shapes a big part of human life. There have been many cases in which smartphones overheat and burst. The designer set out to give buyers a quality handheld device with sharp speed and a crisp display. The design hits its mark as a tool that delivers quality and speed, but it also harms the world, since heavy use can cause overheating. Unlike in past years, designers now face a number of tough calls in planning, creating, and shipping a product. Simple designs, like a can of food, bring ease but can also cause harm through the preservatives in the food. Design has to look past human need and keep safety in view. Because of these stakes, designers are now expected to play more than one role. They are asked to act as therapist, doctor, lawyer, and layperson all at once. Their work is no longer just about a happy buyer. It has become a tool that can make life or break it. The market is also crowded, so designers have to stay sharp to keep up. Instead of designing what society needs, many designers chase a small group of buyers who bring in big returns. In the early 1990s, design was mostly about style. Take furniture, for example. Even when it cost a lot and was not comfortable, people wanted it because it fit the look they wanted for their social standing. Much of the clothing from the 1990s was not comfortable, but people wore it with pride because of the elegance tied to the look. The first iPhone came out in 2007, and it has since sold more than 700 million units, making it one of the best designs of our time.

chapters.title: Designers should aim for meaning over sheer output
chapters.text: The flood of empty and useless design has taken over the work of many designers. Rather than build things that are truly useful, they now make products that are easy to toss. These items are for short term fun, and they have little real use. The time spent on these throwaway pieces could have gone into work that has real value for society. It is easy to buy into a fad, but these items rarely hold any value. They might be hot for a few months, then end up in the bin. A good example is the fidget spinner, sold as a tool to help focus and lower stress. How many do you see now. Not many. The fidget spinner is a perfect case of pointless design and wasted time. You have to pick between making sense and making money if you want to be a designer, as R. Buckminster Fuller once said. Before the days of tight budgets, people changed cars every three years because they were told a car that old was out of date. Because of that, car makers began to build low quality cars that barely lasted three years. In 1977, the American car industry recalled about 10.4 million vehicles even though it had sold only 9.3 million new cars that year. These makers are not pushed to build the best product for the long haul. The short term win of cheap output blinds them. Too many designers crank out new products to make money. Real designers focus on human need and long term use.

chapters.title: Design mistakes can hurt people and the wider world
chapters.text: Every action has a result, and in many cases the result is a negative one. We should also note that being a designer is not easy. Like a surgeon, a designer has no room for error. With heavy duties on their shoulders, designers sometimes fail, and the fallout can be huge. For example, disposable plastic and polyethylene are very useful. They make shopping and carrying goods easy, and they are simple to consume. The intent behind disposables is good, but the harms far outweigh the help. These disposables are usually thrown out in the wrong way, leading to pollution of air, land, and water. There are billions of plastic pieces in the sea, floating and clogging the water. This may be one of the biggest design mistakes in the world. The most important things a designer can do are to spot, isolate, define, and solve problems. Problems come up every day, which is why more designers are needed around the world. Creative thinking means a designer has to be bold enough to step past old limits. In stepping past these limits, designers run into blocks that can hold back their work. These blocks show up as perception blocks, emotional blocks, association blocks, cultural blocks, professional blocks, intellectual blocks, and environmental blocks. To get past these blocks, designers have to pay close attention. They can do this through brainstorming and through fresh, new ways of thinking, using proven tools to ship a top quality design. Some design errors come from powerful myths built on shaky facts. Five myths shape the thinking of many industrial designers today. They are the myth of mass production, the myth of obsolescence, the myth of what people want, the myth of the designer lack of control, and the myth that quality no longer matters. They are myths for a reason, since they are not true. The key thing to remember in this whole talk is that designing products and services for what people need is more important than creating what they want. When you design, make sure it is something that lifts the quality of life. Usefulness and long life are the goal.

chapters.title: Pollution from bad design puts the planet at risk
chapters.text: Pollution cannot be brushed aside when we aim for a healthy world. Across the globe, huge numbers of people have been hurt or disabled by toxic waste. The defoliant Agent Orange scarred the genes of people in Vietnam. Bomb test zones in Nevada and Utah have triggered bone cancer and blood cancer in nearby populations. Every region on earth carries some form of contamination, and with each new wave of growth, the danger grows. Many industrial products are poorly conceived because the makers chase earnings over craft. Pollution cuts deeper than throwaway cups or oily rivers. If we let our guard down, it can threaten the survival of our species. People have cut deep into forests and scraped across deserts. Tree clearing runs far ahead of tree planting, and the public tunes out the environmental warnings issued each year. The result could reshape daily life within a generation, possibly putting human survival itself on the line. To put it plainly, every human pursuit is built on design, because shaping any action toward a chosen outcome is a design exercise, in the words of Victor Papanek. Close to one percent of the remaining tropical canopy disappears every year. Wildlife teeters on the edge of vanishing, while crops struggle because the soil has been saturated with synthetic chemicals. All of this can be dialed back when design is done with care. Designers can play a real role in curbing the damage and stopping new harm. For example, two well known designers named John Maurice and James Aronson built a program that ships young trees abroad for replanting. Through their design, they drove a meaningful boost in forest restoration.

chapters.title: Integrated designers can change the world when profit is not the only goal
chapters.text: Learning should be an experience that brings a sense of joy, according to journalist George B. Leonard. Education for designers is based on learning many things and unlearning others. They should tap into the wisdom that nature shows, instead of following the old teaching methods. Thinking outside the box is key in many situations. When it comes to design, fresh and creative thinking wins over following the same old routines. Bringing together many factors to reach a complete design is key. This process is called integrated design. With integrated design, designers come to see how important it is to bring different parts of life into their choices. Those choices go a long way in shaping the world now and later on. For integrated design to work, designers have to learn several schools of thought. They must know why they should design, when, and what it means for society as a whole. Integrated design takes a full circle view. Designers must understand how a product or service will affect everything and everyone around it. Integrated design calls for a team of strong minds across many fields. The team main focus cannot be profit making. It has to be the order and safety of people and society. Their job is to find problems, isolate them, and then fix them. An integrated design team can be behind a massive change in how we survive. With a growing population and new tools, the world now leans on design to survive more than ever. Car companies are focusing more on reliability and long life, not just looks. The big shift in design ideas is driven by professionals from many fields coming together. Designers should study the impact of design on religion, culture, tradition, and the economy. These studies prepare them to make the right calls on strategy. It is also important to stop making products just to make a profit. The current and next generation of young and hopeful designers is a real source of hope for design. They have seen the impact of design on society, and many want to use their craft to change the world. Design is more than beauty and grace. It is a way of life that deserves careful study.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text: Design is an art, a steady way to create beauty and to protect life. Its true heart is in the planning and the making, and that is why it deserves the closest study. As a designer, you should never lose sight of putting the needs of people first. Victor Papanek was always ahead of his time. He was the first designer to focus on sustainability and on the impact of design on the world. It is easy to crank out products that people think they want, but these often end up in the trash soon after. Wants change all the time, like the wind, but needs tend to stick around for much longer. As a steady and successful designer, it is key to serve the needs of people before you even think about wants. Yes, you can weave in small details to please the wish list, but the focus should be on needs. Doing that is the ethical path, and it also means the item you make will last a long time. The slow and steady path is always better than a huge hit that ends in a steep crash. Too many designers chase the fast buck without thinking about whether what they are making will still be needed a few years, or even a few months, from now. It is also key to think about the impact on the world, since much of the harm that has been done is hard to undo. Try this. Avoid making designs for the sole aim of profit. Make designs because you want to lift the lives of people and improve the world. Work out how to focus on what people need while adding small touches of what they may want. That way you cover all sides. Put together a team of seasoned people who will stand by you through the design process. A few strong minds help you make designs that last. If you have read to the end, well done.
